_source: Actual Mynd emergency criteria provided by Sutton (2026-04-04)

emergency_definitions.condition: No electricity in the entire unit or building
Water leak causing damage to flooring, walls, ceilings, or cabinetry
subconditions: Known weather-related leaks when rainfall forecasted within 24 hours
Unknown or continuous water leaks where source cannot be identified

emergency_definitions.condition: Active fire or evidence of fire
emergency_definitions.action: Resident must contact 911 immediately before Mynd is notified

emergency_definitions.condition: No heat when exterior temp below 55°F or forecasted below 55°F within 3 days

state_exceptions.NV: Heater outage must be addressed within 24 hours

emergency_definitions.condition: No hot water to entire unit
No AC when exterior temp above 85°F or forecasted above 85°F within 3 days

state_exceptions.NV: A/C outage must be addressed within 48 hours

emergency_definitions.note: If unit has multiple systems and only one is down, it is NOT an emergency

emergency_definitions.condition: Toilet, sink or bathtub overflowed with black water or dirty water
Inoperable toilet or shower (if only one bathroom in unit)
emergency_definitions.note: Only emergency if unit has only ONE bathroom

emergency_definitions.condition: Broken window creating safety, security, or weather-exposure risk
emergency_definitions.note: Not cosmetic cracks — must be safety/security/weather risk

emergency_definitions.action: Resident should also contact the utility company

emergency_definitions.condition: Non-functional vehicular access gates preventing safe entry or exit
Garage door inoperable — cannot be manually closed OR vehicle stuck inside
emergency_definitions.note: Emergency only if: can't be manually closed, or can't be manually opened and vehicle is stuck inside

emergency_definitions.condition: Elevator outage — only available elevator or required for ADA access
emergency_definitions.note: Only emergency when it's the only available elevator, or required for ADA access

emergency_definitions.condition: Complete range failure (both oven and stove inoperable)
emergency_definitions.note: Both oven AND stove must be inoperable — if only one is down, not an emergency

emergency_definitions.condition: Unable to secure the unit due to inoperable or broken entry locks

Emergency.description: Life safety, habitability, or active property damage. Immediate response required.

Urgent.description: Significant impact but not life-threatening or causing active damage.

Standard.description: Normal maintenance issue. Schedule within standard windows.
Standard.scheduling: 2+ days out, resident choice of morning (8-12) or afternoon (1-5)

Low.description: Cosmetic or non-critical. Schedule at convenience.
